channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Black boxes from South Korea plane crash failed to record final 4 minutes, officials say
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The black boxes of a Boeing jetliner that crashed in South Korea last month stopped recording about four minutes before the accident, South Korean officials said Saturday, possibly complicating investigations into the cause of the disaster that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As Jean-Marie Le Pen is buried, here's a look at key dates of the rise of the far right in France
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s main far-right party and a polarizing figure in French politics, is being buried Saturday in a private family ceremony in his hometown of La Trinité-sur-Mer in Brittany.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Powerful winter storm dumps heavy snow, causing flight delays, slick roads and school closings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into Southern U.S. states Friday, making for dicey travel and a rare snow day for many students.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected MS-13 gang member nabbed in Texas, charged in El Salvador murder
item.description: Luis Manuel Hernandez Bonilla, a suspected MS-13 gang member, was arrested Tuesday by Texas authorities after a traffic stop and charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Excerpts from President-elect Donald Trump’s sentencing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump spoke for six minutes. The judge talked for seven. In the end, the first criminal court sentencing of a former or future president took little more than half an hour.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: From Carter's funeral, an American snapshot: A singular image, 5 presidents, 379 years of history
item.description: Look at their faces — formal, etched with experience, laden with the weight of momentous decisions. Add up their years: 379 to be exact, enough time to take you back into the mid-1600s, when the notion of the American nation was still more than a century away.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to be sentenced in New York hush money case
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court rejected a last-ditch effort by lawyers for President-elect Donald Trump to prevent his sentencing for his New York criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, KRON4

item.title: Bourbon Street terror victims sue New Orleans as Louisiana AG investigates security lapses
item.description: At least two dozen New Orleans terrorist attack victims are filing lawsuits against the city and its police department, according to the law firms representing them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about thousands of evacuations and homes burned in Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least 10 people were killed and thousands of structures were burned as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anita Bryant, anti-gay rights activist and singer, dead at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant Dry, known popularly Anita Bryant — a singer and crusader against gay rights — has died at the age of 84.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York. The court’s order clears the way for Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: As wildfires burn in LA, what to know about protective masks
item.description: Certain masks are effective in protecting against wildfire smoke — but only if they are fitted and worn properly.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Lisa Kudrow says Matthew Perry left a note for her inside a recognizable 'Friends' prop
item.description: "I hadn't opened it up or looked inside it, but yeah he did. He had a note in there and I forgot about it," Kudrow said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Mexico is a go-to state for women seeking abortions. A new court ruling helps it stay that way
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The New Mexico Supreme Court on Thursday struck down abortion restrictions by conservative cities and counties, helping to ensure the state remains a go-to destination for people from other states with bans.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan woman gets jail after boy brought pink gun to school
item.description: A woman who illegally possessed a pink handgun that a child took to a Grand Rapids elementary school in a backpack has been sentenced to a year in jail.
item.dc:creator: John Hogan, KRON4

item.title: Watchdog groups seeks investigation into RFK Jr. for voting from a 'sham' address
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York election officials are being urged to investigate whether Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indiana man who fled to avoid prison sentence for storming Capitol is arrested in Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Indiana man who fled to avoid serving a nine-month prison sentence for storming the U.S. Capitol was arrested in Canada this week on the fourth anniversary of the mob's attack.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lisa Kudrow says Matthew Perry left a note for her inside a recognizable 'Friends' prop
item.description: "I hadn't opened it up or looked inside it, but yeah he did. He had a note in there and I forgot about it," Kudrow said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Letter from Gov. Cox calls for immediate removal of Salt Lake City ICE director
item.description: Gov. Cox signed a letter requesting changes to local immigration standards and the immediate removal of the ICE director in Salt Lake City.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eaton Fire victim dies holding a hose, defending home
item.description: According to his loved ones, 66-year-old Victor Shaw died trying to defend the home that had been in his family for nearly 55 years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: Water crisis in Central Virginia leaves residents and businesses struggling
item.description: Hanover County remains under a boil water advisory, with no clear timeline from officials on when the situation will return to normal.
item.dc:creator: Sahara Sriraman, NewsNation

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Celebrity homes among those lost in devastating Los Angeles fires
item.description: Wildfires that are burning in and around Los Angeles have burned the homes of several celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Mandy Moore and Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US will keep protecting more than 2,000 Rocky Mountain grizzly bears
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Biden administration said Wednesday it will continue protecting about 2,000 grizzly bears in the Rocky Mountains despite opposition from Republican-led states. Federal officials also said they would ease some restrictions as conflicts between bears and humans rise.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden casts doubt on his fitness to serve another four years days before term ends
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden, in a new interview days before he leaves office, cast doubt on his fitness to serve another four years even as he maintained that he could have won election to a second term.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfires latest: 2 people killed and over 1,000 structures destroyed, per LA fire chief
item.description: Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, fueled by powerful Santa Ana winds, sending residents fleeing from burning homes through flames, ferocious winds and towering clouds of smoke.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New research shows 24% of freshwater animals threatened with extinction
item.description: Nearly a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction, according to new research published Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's woodworking, painting and poetry reveal an introspective Renaissance Man
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — The world knew Jimmy Carter as a president and humanitarian, but he also was a woodworker, painter and poet, creating a body of artistic work that reflects deeply personal views of the global community — and himself.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 fraternity members at San Diego State University charged after one was set on fire during a 'skit': DA
item.description: The incident occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university over issues of hazing and conduct that threatens the health and safety of students.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WGN 9

item.title: Salt Lake City will be one of the most competitive housing markets in 2025, Zillow report predicts
item.description: The hottest housing markets this year are predicted to be on the East Coast, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Scarves over headscarves, Muslim women’s outdoors group tackles snow tubing in Minnesota
item.description: MAPLE GROVE, Minn. (AP) — Ice crystals clung to the eyelashes, parka hood, beanie hat and headscarf of Ruqayah Nasser as she took a break after her first-ever snow tubing runs in a Minnesota park on a subzero (-18 Celsius) January morning.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
